pi_items.source: Mathematical calculation: Base box (200×200×15) + Column cylinder (π×40²×150) + Top platform cylinder (π×70²×12) - Hole cylinder (π×7.5²×177) = 600,000 + 240,000π + 58,800π - 9,956.25π = 600,000 + 288,843.75π ≈ 1,507,429.40 mm³

Geometric analysis: X/Y dimension determined by base box (200×200mm, larger than column diameter 80mm and top platform diameter 140mm); Z dimension is sum of heights: 15mm (base) + 150mm (column) + 12mm (top platform) = 177mm
Task description states: '15mm diameter (7.5mm radius) hole'. Radius = Diameter/2 = 15/2 = 7.5mm
This is a reasonable tolerance value for verifying manufactured/modeled geometry. At 8%, an actual volume between 1,386,834 and 1,627,023 mm³ would pass verification.
This is a reasonable tolerance value for dimensional verification. Allows ±5mm deviation on each axis.
Path specification defined in task.json and verified in export_result.sh script (line 8 and throughout). User 'ga' is defined in env.json with Documents directory under home.

privileged_info_summary: The create_display_pedestal task is entirely synthetic with no external data dependencies. The task requires building a fused 3D solid in FreeCAD comprising: a base box (200×200×15mm), a column cylinder (radius 40mm, height 150mm on top of base), and a top platform cylinder (radius 70mm, height 12mm). These are fused into a single solid with a central hole (radius 7.5mm, height 177mm) cut through for cable routing. Expected geometry properties are: volume 1,507,429 mm³ (verified to be 1,507,429.40 mm³), bounding box [200, 200, 177]mm, with 8% volume tolerance and 5mm dimensional tolerance for verification. All metadata claims have been independently verified through mathematical calculation.
